response:
*KEEP THE AREA CLEAN*  
BRUSH GENTLY AND THOROUGHLY AT LEAST TWICE A DAY WITH A SOFT-BRISTLED TOOTHBRUSH.  
RINSE YOUR MOUTH WITH WATER AFTER EVERY MEAL.  
FLOSS CAREFULLY TO REMOVE FOOD PARTICLES.  

*AVOID CERTAIN FOODS*  
STAY AWAY FROM HARD, STICKY, OR CHEWWY FOODS.  
AVOID EXTREMELY HOT OR COLD FOODS AND BEVERAGES.  
USE TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS AS INSTRUCTIONS SAY.  

*MONITOR FOR CHANGES*  
KEEP AN EYE ON THE TOOTH FOR ANY CHANGES OR SIGNS OF INFECTION.  
WATCH FOR INCREASED PAIN, SENSITIVITY, OR SWELLING.  
SEEK DENTAL CARE IMMEDIATELY IF YOU DEVELOP AN ABSCESS.  

*SEEK DENTAL CARE*  
LOOK FOR DENTAL CARE OPTIONS IN YOUR CURRENT LOCATION.  
DENTAL SCHOOLS, EXPATRIATE COMMUNITIES, OR LOCAL HEALTHCARE SERVICES CAN HELP.  
DON’T WAIT FOR THE FULL DURATION OF YOUR STAY ABROAD.
